skill.name: ace-step
skill.description: Generate, inpaint, and outpaint music with ACE Step models on RunComfy using the runcomfy CLI. The skill documents text-to-audio, multilingual lyric generation, time-range inpainting, and bidirectional outpainting workflows.
skill.summary: Create and edit ACE Step music through RunComfy CLI workflows.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most shell and weak-cryptography hits are Markdown examples or inline code labels, not executable Ruby backticks or cryptographic code. The confirmed risks are intended but meaningful: the skill runs the runcomfy CLI, sends prompts and audio URLs to RunComfy services, and relies on local or environment-based API token handling.

medium_findings.title: Scoped External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to run runcomfy commands for login, text-to-audio generation, inpainting, and outpainting. This is legitimate for the skill purpose and constrained by allowed-tools, but it can submit jobs, create files, and incur service usage if invoked by an agent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command behavior is explicit in the allowed-tools declaration and repeated runcomfy examples. The scope is narrow, so this is an operational risk rather than evidence of arbitrary command execution.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Model API and Media URL Processing
medium_findings.description: The skill sends prompt JSON and audio URL inputs to RunComfy model endpoints and downloads generated audio. This is expected for a cloud music-generation skill, but user prompts, lyrics, and source audio URLs leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly states the CLI POSTs to the RunComfy Model API and uses HTTPS source audio URLs. No evidence shows unauthorized endpoints, but network transfer is inherent to the skill.

medium_findings.title: API Token Storage and Environment Token Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill documents runcomfy login and RUNCOMFY_TOKEN usage, with tokens stored in a hidden configuration path. The text warns against logging tokens, but token exposure remains a moderate concern for shared or poorly isolated environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and environment variable are explicitly documented. The skill includes safe handling guidance, reducing likelihood of misuse but not eliminating credential-handling risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Detections Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Ruby or shell backtick patterns, but the reviewed locations are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, endpoint names, schema field names, or documentation tables. They do not represent Ruby backtick execution in source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown documentation, not executable program files. Some fenced command examples are real CLI examples, but the Ruby-backtick classification is incorrect.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high blocker hits align with descriptive prose, music tags, comparison tables, and exit-code documentation rather than cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance commands. No evidence of weak hashing, encryption, or host probing was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found music-generation documentation and tables, not security-sensitive primitives. The scanner appears to have matched short substrings out of context.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern: runcomfy CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill authorizes Bash(runcomfy *) and provides runcomfy run examples. The command pattern is required for the skill, but marketplace users should understand it executes an external CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool scope and implementation summary directly identify runcomfy CLI execution. No broader shell pattern is present in the reviewed skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Pattern: RunComfy API Submission
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill states that the CLI posts to the RunComfy Model API, polls request status, and downloads generated audio. This is expected behavior but means prompts and media references are processed by a remote service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is described plainly in the skill. The documented allowlist reduces suspicion, but it remains a publish-time warning.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Pattern: Hidden Token File
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents that runcomfy login stores the API token under a hidden configuration path with mode 0600. This is a standard token storage pattern, but it requires careful handling on shared systems.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact token path and permissions are documented. The same line includes mitigation guidance, so the concern is credential handling rather than confirmed leakage.

content.user_title: Generate ACE Step Music on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Music teams need fast drafts, multilingual vocals, and time-based edits without high per-second costs. This skill guides ACE Step generation, inpainting, and outpainting through the RunComfy CLI.
actual_capabilities: Selects ACE Step text-to-audio or ACE Step 1.5 based on lyric and language needs.
Builds tag-driven music prompts with genre, mood, instruments, BPM, and lyric markers.
Runs audio inpainting workflows for a specified start and end time range.
Runs audio outpainting workflows to add intro, outro, or both around an existing track.
Explains duration, seed, lyric, audio URL, and output directory parameters for RunComfy jobs.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and an authenticated RunComfy account or token.
Sends prompts, lyrics, and source audio URLs to RunComfy cloud services.
Each ACE Step call is limited to four minutes of generated or edited audio.
Does not verify rights for user-supplied lyrics, melodies, or source recordings.

use_cases.title: Draft Background Tracks
use_cases.description: Generate low-cost instrumental loops and mood beds from tags before selecting final tracks.

use_cases.title: Localize Vocal Concepts
use_cases.description: Use ACE Step 1.5 with structured lyrics to test vocal versions across many languages.

use_cases.title: Repair or Extend Audio
use_cases.description: Regenerate a weak section or extend a short hook without rebuilding the entire song.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Instrumental Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ACE Step to generate a 60 second instrumental track. Style: lo-fi hip-hop, mellow, rhodes piano, soft drums, 75 BPM. Save it to ./out.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Structured Vocal Song
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90 second ACE Step 1.5 song with verse, chorus, bridge, and outro markers. Use indie pop tags and female vocal energy.

prompt_templates.title: Replace a Time Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ACE Step audio-inpaint on this HTTPS audio URL. Replace seconds 20 through 40 with a softer piano bridge and no vocals.

prompt_templates.title: Extend a Hook Into a Track
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ACE Step audio-outpaint to add a 30 second intro and 60 second outro to this hook URL. Match the indie pop tags and keep lyrics instrumental.

output_examples.input: Request: Generate a mellow instrumental loop for a menu screen.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects ACE Step base, prepares specific tags, sets instrumental lyrics, chooses a short duration, and saves the generated file.

output_examples.input: Request: Fix the final chorus in an existing song URL.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses audio-inpaint, targets the requested time range, keeps matching style tags, and explains that only that section is regenerated.

output_examples.input: Request: Turn a 30 second hook into a longer arrangement.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses audio-outpaint, adds time before and after the source, and downloads the extended result into the output folder.

best_practices: Use precise tags for genre, mood, instruments, tempo, vocal style, and language.
Use fixed seeds when comparing prompt changes or recreating a successful draft.
Confirm rights for user-supplied lyrics and source audio before generation or editing.
anti_patterns: Do not send private lyrics, unreleased recordings, or confidential audio URLs without approval.
Do not use broad tags like good music when tempo, style, and instrumentation matter.
Do not run long batches before testing short drafts and expected service cost.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates or edits music with ACE Step models through the RunComfy CLI.

faq.question: Does it support vocals?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports structured lyrics, and ACE Step 1.5 is documented for 50 or more languages.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing track?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inpaint a time range or outpaint before and after an existing audio URL.

faq.question: Does it run local model inference?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses the RunComfy CLI to submit jobs to RunComfy services.

faq.question: What credentials are needed?
faq.answer: RunComfy authentication is needed through runcomfy login or the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is safe to publish with a medium-risk warning for CLI execution, network use, and token handling.
